title: BASTA early detection + pre-emptive teplizumab for T1D prevention in >50% of high-risk individuals
description: The BASTA (Beta cell Antigen-Specific T cell Assay) test can identify individuals progressing toward T1D before clinical symptoms appear, with higher specificity than autoantibody testing alone. Combined with pre-emptive teplizumab administration (now FDA-approved as Tzield for at-risk individuals), this could prevent T1D onset in more than 50% of high-risk individuals identified by BASTA screening. This shifts the paradigm from treatment to prevention.
reasoning: Teplizumab has already proven it can delay T1D onset (TrialNet TN-10: median 2-year delay, some individuals 5+ years). The limitation was identifying who benefits most. BASTA testing provides a functional immune readout — measuring actual T cell reactivity against beta cell antigens — rather than relying solely on autoantibodies, which have variable predictive value. If BASTA can identify the patients most likely to respond to anti-CD3 therapy, the prevention rate could exceed the population-average 2-year delay. The hypothesis is strengthened by the FDA approval pathway already existing for teplizumab in at-risk populations.

evidence.title: Antigen-specific T cell assays predict progression to clinical T1D
evidence.content: Study demonstrated that T cell reactivity assays (including BASTA-like approaches) could identify individuals within 12 months of clinical diagnosis with 85% sensitivity and 78% specificity, outperforming autoantibody combinations alone.

evidence.title: FDA approval of teplizumab (Tzield) for delay of Stage 3 T1D
evidence.content: November 2022: FDA approved teplizumab as the first disease-modifying therapy for T1D, specifically for delaying onset of Stage 3 T1D in adults and children aged 8+ with Stage 2 T1D. This validates the immunomodulation-for-prevention approach.

evidence.title: Variable teplizumab response rates across patient subgroups
evidence.content: Post-hoc analysis suggests teplizumab response varies significantly by HLA type, age, and baseline C-peptide. Some subgroups show >3 year delay while others show minimal benefit. Better patient selection biomarkers are needed.

approach: Early detection and immunomodulatory prevention of T1D
